residual_risks: Icon.vue's ICONS map hand-transcribes SVG path data from bootstrap-icons v1.11.0; if that upstream library is later bumped elsewhere (e.g. the CDN removal in v0.6.0 U8 mentioned in comments), nothing enforces the inline paths stay in sync with a newer icon set version — drift would be silent since there's no build-time extraction step.

ConfirmDialog's Escape handler is registered in the capture phase and calls stopPropagation specifically to out-race useSidenavDrawer's bubble-phase listener; this ordering contract is documented only in comments, not in a test or shared constant, so a future refactor of the drawer's listener phase could silently break the 'topmost dialog closes first' guarantee.

BloggerCard's doRevoke is designed to never reject (errors are caught internally and routed to toastError) so that ConfirmDialog's inline-error path is effectively dead code for this call site; this is intentional per the inline comment, but a future maintainer wiring a new confirm callback by copying BloggerCard's pattern may not realize the dialog's own error-recovery path exists and is exercised elsewhere (KeepAlivePage does not use it either — flashMessage is used instead).
testing_gaps: No test appears to cover ConfirmDialog's focus-trap Tab/Shift+Tab cycling or focus-restoration-to-trigger-element behavior directly in ConfirmDialog.spec.ts scope beyond what was skimmed; given the a11y guarantees are a documented contract (focus trap, Escape capture ordering vs. sidenav drawer), a dedicated interaction test with a real sidenav drawer mounted alongside would give higher confidence the capture-phase/stopPropagation contract holds across both components simultaneously, not just in isolation.
